QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_66

question.text: Telephone recording of client order instructions is mandatory when orders are received through:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Per SEBI circular dated March 22, 2018, wherever order instructions are received from clients through telephone, the stock broker shall mandatorily use telephone recording system to record the instructions and maintain telephone recordings as part of records. **The Trap:** For internet orders, the system log serves as evidence. For written orders, the physical document signed by the client serves as evidence. Telephone is the only channel requiring mandatory recording. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers order evidence requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Order evidence must exist for EVERY trade — form depends on channel: (a) Written/signed physical record, (b) Telephone recording, (c) Email from authorized ID, (d) Internet transaction log, (e) SMS record, (f) Any legally verifiable record. Without evidence, broker faces disputes without defense.
